questions.q: What does the abbreviation HSRA stand for?
questions.explanation: HSRA stands for the Hindustan Socialist Republican Association, a revolutionary organization of the freedom struggle.

questions.q: In which year was the organization reorganized and renamed the Hindustan Socialist Republican Association?
questions.explanation: The HSRA was formed in 1928 when the earlier Hindustan Republican Association was reorganized.

questions.q: The HSRA was a reorganized form of which earlier revolutionary body?
questions.explanation: The HSRA grew out of the Hindustan Republican Association (HRA), which had carried out the Kakori action.

questions.q: Which leader served as the commander-in-chief of the HSRA?
questions.explanation: Chandrashekhar Azad led the HSRA as its commander-in-chief after its 1928 reorganization.

questions.q: What word added to the HRA's name reflected the new socialist ideology of the HSRA?
questions.explanation: The word 'Socialist' was added to mark the organization's adoption of a socialist goal for a free India.

questions.q: Which famous young revolutionary was a leading member and ideologue of the HSRA?
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh was among the foremost leaders and ideologues of the HSRA.

questions.q: What kind of state did the HSRA ultimately aim to establish in India?
questions.explanation: The HSRA sought complete independence and the creation of a socialist republic.

questions.q: In which country did the HSRA operate as a revolutionary organization?
questions.explanation: The HSRA operated within British India, primarily in the north of the country.

questions.q: Which method did the HSRA chiefly favour to win independence?
questions.explanation: The HSRA pursued independence through revolutionary and armed means rather than constitutional methods.

questions.q: Three HSRA members were executed together in Lahore on 23 March 1931. Who were they?
questions.explanation: HSRA members Bhagat Singh, Rajguru and Sukhdev were hanged at Lahore on 23 March 1931.

questions.q: At which Delhi location did revolutionaries meet in September 1928 to form the HSRA?
questions.explanation: The HSRA was constituted at a meeting held at the Feroz Shah Kotla ruins in Delhi in September 1928.

questions.q: The HSRA assassinated which British police officer in Lahore in December 1928?
questions.explanation: HSRA members shot Assistant Superintendent John Saunders in Lahore in December 1928, mistaking him for Scott.

questions.q: The Saunders killing was carried out to avenge the death of which nationalist leader?
questions.explanation: The HSRA killed Saunders to avenge Lala Lajpat Rai, who had died after a lathi charge during a Simon Commission protest.

questions.q: In which 1929 action did HSRA members throw bombs inside a legislative chamber 'to make the deaf hear'?
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh and Batukeshwar Dutt threw bombs in the Central Legislative Assembly in April 1929.

questions.q: Which HSRA member accompanied Bhagat Singh in throwing bombs in the Central Legislative Assembly in 1929?
questions.explanation: Batukeshwar Dutt joined Bhagat Singh in throwing bombs in the Assembly and was arrested with him.

questions.q: Which HSRA member died after a prolonged hunger strike in jail in 1929?
questions.explanation: Jatindra Nath Das died in September 1929 after a hunger strike of over sixty days demanding better treatment for political prisoners.

questions.q: Which document, often attributed to HSRA members, was famously titled 'Why I Am an Atheist'?
questions.explanation: Bhagat Singh of the HSRA wrote the essay 'Why I Am an Atheist' while in jail in 1930.

questions.q: Where did HSRA commander Chandrashekhar Azad die in a gun battle in February 1931?
questions.explanation: Azad died fighting police at Alfred Park in Allahabad in February 1931, shooting himself to avoid capture.

questions.q: Which slogan was popularized by HSRA revolutionaries during their trials and protests?
questions.explanation: HSRA revolutionaries, especially Bhagat Singh, popularized the slogan 'Inquilab Zindabad' meaning 'Long Live the Revolution'.

questions.q: The 1928 reorganization meeting drew members chiefly from which two regions?
questions.explanation: The HSRA's members were drawn largely from Punjab and the United Provinces (modern Uttar Pradesh).

questions.q: Which earlier 1925 action of the predecessor HRA helped inspire the HSRA's revolutionary tradition?
questions.explanation: The Hindustan Republican Association carried out the 1925 Kakori train robbery, part of the lineage the HSRA continued.

questions.q: Which HSRA bomb-maker is credited with helping prepare the devices used in the group's actions?
questions.explanation: Bhagwati Charan Vohra was an HSRA member involved in preparing bombs; he died testing one in 1930.

questions.q: Who is credited as the principal author of the HSRA manifesto 'The Philosophy of the Bomb' (1930)?
questions.explanation: Bhagwati Charan Vohra is generally credited as the chief author of 'The Philosophy of the Bomb', issued in reply to Gandhi's criticism.

questions.q: The HSRA's 1929 Assembly bombing was a protest against which two proposed bills?
options: The Rowlatt Bill and the Press Bill
The Vernacular Press Bill and the Arms Bill
The Salt Bill and the Sedition Bill
The Public Safety Bill and the Trade Disputes Bill
questions.explanation: The Assembly bombing protested the Public Safety Bill and the Trade Disputes Bill being pushed through the legislature.

questions.q: Which conspiracy case tried HSRA members for the Saunders killing and Assembly bombing?
questions.explanation: HSRA members were tried in the Lahore Conspiracy Case, which led to the death sentences for Bhagat Singh, Rajguru and Sukhdev.

questions.q: Which HSRA-linked revolutionary attempted to bomb the Viceroy Lord Irwin's train near Delhi in December 1929?
options: Members of the HSRA including Yashpal
questions.explanation: HSRA revolutionaries, with Yashpal among those involved, attempted to bomb the Viceroy's train near Delhi in December 1929.

questions.q: Which HSRA member later became a noted Hindi novelist and wrote 'Simhavalokan' recounting the revolutionary years?
questions.explanation: Yashpal, an HSRA member, became a major Hindi writer and chronicled the period in works including 'Simhavalokan'.

questions.q: The HSRA reorganization in 1928 was prompted partly by the loss of which earlier leaders to the gallows?
options: Ram Prasad Bismil and Ashfaqulla Khan
questions.explanation: The HRA leaders Ram Prasad Bismil and Ashfaqulla Khan were executed in 1927 after Kakori, prompting younger members to regroup as the HSRA.

questions.q: In which year did Bhagwati Charan Vohra die while testing a bomb on the banks of the Ravi?
questions.explanation: Bhagwati Charan Vohra died in 1930 when a bomb he was testing near the Ravi river exploded.

questions.q: Besides Punjab and the UP, which leader connected the HSRA with revolutionary circles in Bengal?
questions.explanation: Jatindra Nath Das, a Bengali revolutionary, joined the HSRA's circle and was central to its hunger strike in Lahore jail.
